# majorsilence-reporting

> Python library for generating reports (PDF, Excel, CSV, HTML, RTF, TIFF, and more)
> from RDL report definitions. Wraps the Majorsilence Reporting .NET engine.
> No .NET runtime required when using the bundled native library wheel.

## When to use this library

Use `majorsilence-reporting` when you need to:
- Generate PDF, Excel, or other formatted reports from Python
- Render RDL (Report Definition Language) report files
- Produce reports without a .NET runtime on the host (use the native wheel)
- Stream report output as bytes for HTTP responses (Flask, Django, FastAPI, etc.)
- Supply data entirely from Python code — no database connection required

## Installation

```bash
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate   # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
pip install majorsilence-reporting
```

Platform-specific wheels bundle the prebuilt `rdlnative` shared library:
- Linux x64 / arm64
- Windows x64
- macOS arm64

On supported platforms `pip install` is all that is needed — no extra downloads.

## Three usage modes

### 1. Native in-process (recommended — no .NET runtime, lowest overhead)

```python
from majorsilence_reporting import load_bundled_library
from majorsilence_reporting.report_native import Report

lib = load_bundled_library()          # uses the library bundled in the wheel
rpt = Report(lib, "/path/to/report.rdl")
rpt.set_connection_string("Data Source=/path/to/database.db")
rpt.set_parameter("Country", "Germany")

rpt.export("pdf", "/path/to/output.pdf")   # save to file
data = rpt.export_to_memory("pdf")          # returns bytes (for HTTP streaming)
```

### 2. AOT / self-contained subprocess (no .NET runtime required)

```python
from majorsilence_reporting import ReportAot

rpt = ReportAot("/path/to/report.rdl", "/path/to/RdlCmd")
rpt.set_connection_string("Data Source=/path/to/database.db")
rpt.export("pdf", "/path/to/output.pdf")
```

### 3. .NET runtime subprocess

```python
from majorsilence_reporting import Report

rpt = Report("/path/to/report.rdl", "/path/to/RdlCmd.dll", path_to_dotnet="dotnet")
rpt.set_connection_string("Data Source=/path/to/database.db")
rpt.export("pdf", "/path/to/output.pdf")
```

## Supplying data from Python (no database needed)

```python
lib = load_bundled_library()
rpt = Report(lib, "/path/to/report.rdl")
rpt.add_data("DataSetName", [
    {"Product": "Widget A", "Amount": "1250.00"},
    {"Product": "Widget B", "Amount":  "980.50"},
])
data = rpt.export_to_memory("pdf")
```

Dict keys must exactly match `<Field Name="...">` values in the RDL file.
No connection string needed — `SkipDatabaseSchemaValidation` is set automatically.

## Supported export formats

| Format string | Output |
|---|---|
| `pdf` | PDF (default) |
| `xlsx` | Excel workbook |
| `xlsx_table` | Excel workbook (table style) |
| `csv` | Comma-separated values |
| `html` | HTML |
| `mht` | MHTML |
| `rtf` | Rich Text Format |
| `xml` | XML |
| `tif` | TIFF image |
| `tifb` | TIFF image (black & white) |

## Key API reference

### `load_bundled_library() -> ctypes.CDLL`
Loads the rdlnative shared library bundled in the platform wheel. Call once per process.
Raises `FileNotFoundError` if no bundled library is present (pure-Python install).

### `load_library(lib_path: str) -> ctypes.CDLL`
Loads rdlnative from an explicit file path. Use when bundled library is unavailable.

### `report_native.Report(lib, report_path)`
In-process report renderer. Methods:
- `set_parameter(name, value)` — set a report parameter
- `set_connection_string(connection_string)` — override the RDL connection string
- `add_data(dataset_name, rows)` — supply in-memory data (list of dicts)
- `export(format, output_path)` — render to file
- `export_to_memory(format) -> bytes` — render to bytes (no temp file)

### `ReportAot(report_path, rdl_cmd_path)`
Subprocess runner for AOT/self-contained RdlCmd binaries.
Same `set_parameter`, `set_connection_string`, `export`, `export_to_memory` interface.

### `Report(report_path, rdl_cmd_path, path_to_dotnet=None)`
Subprocess runner requiring a .NET runtime.
Same interface as `ReportAot`.

## Resources

- RDL report files and sample databases: https://github.com/majorsilence/Reporting/tree/master/Examples
- Native binary releases: https://github.com/majorsilence/Reporting/releases
- Source for these Python bindings: https://github.com/majorsilence/reporting-python
